title: Top eco-friendly kitchen design tips for a sustainable space
description: LIFESTYLE NEWS - Climate change is becoming...
bodyText:
LIFESTYLE NEWS - Climate change is becoming an increasingly important issue that affects everyone.

As a result, many homeowners are seeking ways to make their homes more eco-friendly.

There are many ways to make your kitchen more sustainable, from the materials and construction methods used in the build to energy-efficient appliances and long-term sustainability considerations.


> Ways to be sustainable in the kitchen


Reclaim and reuseWhen possible, start to reclaim and reuse materials in your kitchen design. If you can use recycled stone for your kitchen worktops or reclaimed wood for your cabinets.

Reclaiming and reusing materials is a great way to reduce waste and cut down on manufacturing emissions while also creating a unique, sustainable kitchen.

Opt for green appliancesA majority of global appliance companies are embracing sustainable living by designing eco-friendly appliances. Select energy-efficient appliances such as toasters and refrigerators that feature measuring scales and automatically switch off to save energy.

Although they might be more expensive, they are, however, an investment. So shop around for more energy-efficient appliances to help the environment.

Cutting down on plasticPlastic is one of the most widely used materials in the kitchen. It is also one of the most harmful to the environment, especially single-use plastic items.


> If possible, avoid using plastic storage containers and opt for glass or stainless steel instead. Use these alternatives to preserve food for you and your family.


Find ways to conserve waterWater conservation is another key component of an eco-friendly kitchen. Installing low-flow faucets and water-saving dishwashers can significantly reduce water usage. The kitchen uses water for various reasons.

These fixtures are designed to use less water while maintaining strong performance. This, in turn, helps to lower your water bills and reduce your environmental impact.

Zero-waste kitchens focus on reducing waste and promoting recycling and composting. To save the planet, these trends have become more common.

They highlight the growing importance of sustainability in many homes.

Compiled by: Chumasanda Matiwane

First published by Garden & Home

The post Top eco-friendly kitchen design tips for a sustainable space appeared first on Bona Magazine.

Article: Caxton Network News
